advantages: Train and predict in linear time over vocabulary size—ideal for high-volume text pipelines on CPU.
Provide interpretable per-word log-probabilities and log-odds for error analysis and compliance review.
Work well with small labeled datasets where deep models overfit; strong baseline before neural fine-tuning.
Numerically stable in log space; closed-form counting updates without iterative optimization.
disadvantages: Bag-of-words features discard word order and context—"not good" and "good" may be misread without n-grams or negation handling.
Conditional independence is violated in real text (synonyms, phrases), capping accuracy versus contextual models.
Large vocabularies produce high-dimensional sparse matrices; memory and calibration suffer without pruning or hashing.
Bernoulli NB can overweight absence on long documents; Multinomial NB can overweight frequent function words without stop-word or TF–IDF treatment.
